rulings.Case Title: DEEANNA M. BROSSOIT VS. DREXEL BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 21, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT DREXEL BRADSHAW, BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Plaintiffs late opposition considered. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must caption each cause of action identifying which defendant it refers to. (California Rule of Court 2.112.) As to the 1st cause of action for legal malpractice, plaintiff must clearly allege what duty defendants owed to plaintiff, how defendants breached the duty, and that plaintiff would have prevailed in the underlying law suit. As to the 2nd cause of action for fraud, plaintiff must allege with specificity that defendant knew his representations to remove minor's counsel were false and allege damages with particularity. As to the 3rd cause of action for negligence misrepresentation, plaintiff must allege why defendant had no reasonable grounds to believe his representations were true and allege specific damages. As to the 4th cause of action, plaintiff must state whether the contract was oral, written or implied by conduct, the material terms of the contract, how defendants breached the contract, and allege that she fulfilled her obligations and complied with the terms and conditions of the contract. Plaintiff must describe a measurable injury or damages resulting from the breach. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NORTEK PRODUCTS (TAICHANG) LIMITED, et al VS. IP CLEANING S.P.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Jurisdiction;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 21, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANT IP CLEANING S.P.A. Motion To Dismiss for Lack of Jurisdiction. Granted. Plaintiff fails to establish that this court has personal jurisdiction over defendant in either form, general or specific. Defendant does not have substantial, systematic, and continuous ties to California to support general jurisdiction. Defendant is a foreign corporation as are all the parties. No showing that defendant has property, offices, facilities, employees, or bank accounts in California, or that defendant maintains marketing efforts in California. No specific jurisdiction: the contract was negotiated outside of California and provides all disputes are to be governed by Italian law and any unresolved controversy shall be exclusively settled by the Court of Milan. A motion to compel will not alter the outcome of this motion. Defendant would likely incur significant expense in defending this action in California. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MEADOWS OF ESTERO-BONITA SPRINGS LIMITED et al VS. SHELBY PARTNER AT MEADOWS, INC., A FLORIDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Robert Shelley Revocable Trust'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 21, 2010, line 8, Defendant the Robert Shelley Revocable Trust's Motion to Quash Service of Summons for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction. Is granted. Insufficient showing that SRT has itself, or through Shelley acting as trustee, purposefully availed itself of the California forum. See Hanson vs. Denckla (1958) 357 US 235. =(302/CWW)
